title: The Set Up

jokes.text: Brooks Brothers. Bought it right off the mannequin.
jokes.setup: Tom boasting about his fashion sense in response to question about Justin
jokes.punchline: Reveals his literal approach to buying clothes - just took the whole mannequin display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - his vanity and materialistic nature coming through in an unexpectedly literal way

jokes.text: I've been getting a lot of visitors recently, thanks to a stupid and worthless new push to make government officials more accessible to the public.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he's getting visitors
jokes.punchline: His disdain for public accessibility - the very purpose of government
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Ron - hating the basic function of his job with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: I made this in one of your pottery classes. It's terrible.
jokes.setup: Citizen presenting pottery to Ron
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of their own terrible work
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan moment

jokes.text: This is my hell.
jokes.setup: Ron dealing with citizens all day
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assessment of his situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction - maximum drama with minimum words

jokes.text: No dressing, no cheese, no croutons, no taste.
jokes.setup: Listing everything removed from Tom's salad
jokes.punchline: Adding 'no taste' as the logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with perfect timing

jokes.text: Salami on pumpernickel with olive juice and extra iceberg.
jokes.setup: Describing the sandwich named after her
jokes.punchline: The combination sounds absolutely disgusting
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's questionable taste in food through absurd sandwich combination

jokes.text: I just wish I'd liked it.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her named sandwich
jokes.punchline: Admitting she doesn't even like her own sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - finding the silver lining even when there isn't one

jokes.text: I asked the police department if I could have it and they said no, so I stole it.
jokes.setup: Leslie wanting Dave's police photo
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to theft after being denied
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from sweet to criminal with Leslie's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Although that's pretty much why I broke up with Andy. He lived way too close to me.
jokes.setup: April sympathizing about long-distance relationships
jokes.punchline: Her opposite problem - Andy lived too close
jokes.explanation: Perfect April subversion - takes a relatable problem and flips it with her antisocial personality

jokes.text: So?
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out there's no paper in the printer
jokes.punchline: April's complete indifference to this problem
jokes.explanation: Pure April - maximum apathy in minimum words

jokes.text: In the eight years I've been at the job, I've saved the taxpayers of this city more than 150 grand. But now, I need the taxpayers' money to save me from the taxpayers.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his frugality with government money
jokes.punchline: The ironic reversal of needing taxpayer money to avoid taxpayers
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Ron's predicament with government irony

jokes.text: This looks like an ad for nothing.
jokes.setup: Ron giving April his assistant job posting
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment of his vague posting
jokes.explanation: April's directness cutting through Ron's deliberately vague job posting

jokes.text: Please be careful with it, it's my only copy.
jokes.setup: Ron handing April the job posting
jokes.punchline: His paranoia about a simple piece of paper
jokes.explanation: Shows Ron's old-school approach to technology/copying

jokes.text: Is a friend of mine! That's my motto, too.
jokes.setup: Justin saying 'Any friend of Ann's...'
jokes.punchline: Leslie's overly enthusiastic completion and claiming it as her motto
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to over-connect and be overly friendly

jokes.text: Is it locking up the bad guys or keeping them on the streets?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about Justin's legal specialty
jokes.punchline: Her oversimplified view of law as purely criminal
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's naive understanding of legal profession

jokes.text: it was invented to warm up baby bottles.
jokes.setup: Describing a dangerous blowtorch product
jokes.punchline: Its innocent intended purpose
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist contrast between dangerous product and innocent purpose

jokes.text: You are wearing the hell out of that suit, sir. Banana, two-button.
jokes.setup: Tom meeting Justin
jokes.punchline: His immediate focus on and expertise about fashion details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - immediately assessing and complimenting fashion with specific details

jokes.text: I come in, like, two hours late because I had to finish watching Swimfan because it was on, and everyone, like, jumped down my throat.
jokes.setup: Student complaining about work criticism
jokes.punchline: His completely unjustifiable excuse for being late
jokes.explanation: Good millennial entitlement joke with specific pop culture reference

jokes.text: I have been writing a lot of songs about shining shoes lately. I tend to write about what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his musical process
jokes.punchline: His literal approach to songwriting inspiration
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - taking something artistic and making it completely literal

jokes.text: Talking about writing about what I'm doing. Now I'm singing about talking about writing about what I'm doing
jokes.setup: Andy's literal songwriting approach
jokes.punchline: Getting caught in an increasingly meta loop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and meta-commentary that could only come from Andy's simple mind

jokes.text: He has the brains of George Clooney in the body of Joe Biden.
jokes.setup: Ann asking about Leslie's ideal man
jokes.punchline: Her backwards and politically obsessed description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - politically obsessed and getting conventional attractiveness completely backwards

jokes.text: Interesting. That's uh, weird.
jokes.setup: Leslie's bizarre description of ideal man
jokes.punchline: Ann's diplomatic but honest reaction
jokes.explanation: Good straight-person reaction to Leslie's weirdness

jokes.text: I don't want to date a twin, 'cause I've been tricked before.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ann dating rules
jokes.punchline: Revealing a mysterious and concerning past experience
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy moment that raises more questions than it answers

jokes.text: Ron Solo!
jokes.setup: Tom greeting Ron
jokes.punchline: His Star Wars reference nickname for Ron
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's pop culture obsession and attempt to be cool

jokes.text: Yeah, my friends know that I have a strict no call policy.
jokes.setup: Tom asking how Ron knows caller was public vs friend
jokes.punchline: Ron's antisocial friendship rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - even his friendships have bureaucratic rules

jokes.text: Oh, damn, now I have to go back.
jokes.setup: Ann noticing something on herself
jokes.punchline: Realizing she has blood on her from work
jokes.explanation: Simple nurse humor about work hazards

jokes.text: Well done, Ann. I actually heard that.
jokes.setup: Leslie complimenting Ann on the setup
jokes.punchline: Chris overhearing the behind-the-scenes conversation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of behind-the-scenes talk being overheard

jokes.text: You know, isn't it funny how these setups are always just so awkward?
jokes.setup: Chris trying to break tension on the date
jokes.punchline: His comment actually making things more awkward
jokes.explanation: Self-fulfilling prophecy of awkwardness - meta commentary that backfires

jokes.text: I will now extinguish this candle with my bare hand.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to impress Leslie with a 'trick'
jokes.punchline: The obvious pain that follows
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup with predictable but effective payoff

jokes.text: It looked so much easier on the Internet.
jokes.setup: Chris burning himself trying to put out candle
jokes.punchline: His reference to learning this 'trick' online
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern humor about Internet tutorials vs reality

jokes.text: Oh no thanks. I'm not thirsty.
jokes.setup: Chris asking to borrow ice for his burned hand
jokes.punchline: Leslie thinking he's offering her ice to drink
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication joke showing Leslie's politeness overriding context

jokes.text: He practically saved my life. He was, like, the only cool dude at my high school.
jokes.setup: Ann describing her relationship with Justin
jokes.punchline: Her hyperbolic language revealing her feelings
jokes.explanation: Shows Ann's denial about her feelings through overstatement

jokes.text: I got a nightclub opening to go to. Which tie do I wear?
jokes.setup: Tom asking job candidate for advice
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate question for a job interview
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - turning a job interview into personal fashion consultation

jokes.text: That was a trick question. The answer's this one.
jokes.setup: Tom showing candidate two ties
jokes.punchline: Revealing a third, 'correct' tie was hidden
jokes.explanation: Great Tom moment showing his fashion obsession and dramatic flair

jokes.text: Did you ever see that movie The Matrix? 99% real.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing to 'interview' candidate
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous conspiracy theory statement
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's tendency toward conspiracy theories and pop culture obsession

jokes.text: or the potatoes or this candle or this beer or this flower or this bread.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining MRI capabilities
jokes.punchline: His obsessive listing of random objects
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chris's weird obsession with looking inside things

jokes.text: Yeah but what's inside that bread? Could be cheese.
jokes.setup: Leslie pointing out bread just contains more bread
jokes.punchline: Chris's illogical but confident response
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic from Chris showing his weird thought process

jokes.text: Frankly, I don't want to invest in someone who only has three months to live.
jokes.setup: Chris offering Leslie a free MRI
jokes.punchline: His weirdly mercenary view of relationships
jokes.explanation: Dark and weird joke that reveals Chris's disturbing approach to dating

jokes.text: And how creepy their grandfather was.
jokes.setup: Chris talking about telling grandkids about their weird first date
jokes.punchline: Leslie's pointed addition about Chris being creepy
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie comeback that cuts right to the truth

jokes.text: Sometimes smiling is read as AIDS.
jokes.setup: Chris telling Leslie not to smile during MRI
jokes.punchline: His completely nonsensical medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd fake medical advice that's perfectly in character for creepy Chris

jokes.text: I wait eight weeks and I text her, 'What's cracking?'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his dating strategy to candidate
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous delay and casual text
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom dating advice that's completely backwards and trying too hard to be cool

jokes.text: Covering for me at any legally dicey situations.
jokes.setup: Tom listing assistant duties
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning illegal activity coverage
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's casual attitude toward questionable activities

jokes.text: In the immortal words of Rod Blagojevich, 'It's a (fucking) valuable thing. You just don't give it away for nothing.'
jokes.setup: Tom justifying his headhunter fee
jokes.punchline: Quoting a corrupt politician as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Great topical reference showing Tom's questionable moral compass and business sense

jokes.text: You got a big, industrial-sized oven.
jokes.setup: Chris commenting on Leslie's reproductive anatomy via MRI
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate kitchen metaphor
jokes.explanation: Incredibly cringe-worthy line that perfectly captures Chris's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Leslie is a notoriously cruel and thoughtless person.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining why she didn't set Leslie up with Justin
jokes.punchline: Mark's sarcastic description of famously kind Leslie
jokes.explanation: Perfect sarcastic comment highlighting the absurdity of Ann's excuse

jokes.text: We almost wheeled you out of here in a chair.
jokes.setup: Chris finding the 'brain shadow' was just a smudge
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overstatement of what would have happened
jokes.explanation: Shows Chris's tendency toward dramatic overstatement

jokes.text: Imagine if you had spent rest of your life sipping a steak through a straw.
jokes.setup: Chris talking about Leslie almost being disabled
jokes.punchline: His bizarre specific image of disability
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird and specific image that's both absurd and slightly offensive

jokes.text: and I'll wait respectful amount of time. I don't know, maybe?
jokes.setup: Ann explaining hypothetical scenario where she might date Justin
jokes.punchline: Her casual consideration of Mark's tragic death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about planning for boyfriend's death to date someone else

jokes.text: I should have noticed that you're missing a heart.
jokes.setup: Chris saying goodbye after failed date
jokes.punchline: His attempt at a cruel parting shot
jokes.explanation: Predictable 'missing heart' insult but fits Chris's character

jokes.text: Hey, do you mind if we pick up my son Nate at his basketball game on the way back?
jokes.setup: Chris and Leslie leaving the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Chris casually revealing he has a son after this disastrous date
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation - worst possible time to reveal major personal information

jokes.text: Ooh you slept together? What? Not sexually. God what's wrong with you, Tom?
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining she got an MRI
jokes.punchline: Tom's interpretation and Leslie's horrified correction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Tom's one-track mind

jokes.text: Seems like the shoe is in the other foot. And the guy who polishes shoes is now me.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on role reversal with April
jokes.punchline: His confused mixing of metaphors
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - getting idioms wrong while trying to sound smart

jokes.text: I would say, in terms of guys that she's in love with, Justin's up here, I'm up here, and then you're down here.
jokes.setup: Andy ranking Ann's romantic interests
jokes.punchline: Putting himself equal with Justin above Mark
jokes.explanation: Good Andy delusion about his place in Ann's affections

jokes.text: Well, I just wanted to make sure that I wasn't crazy, so, naturally, I came to you for reassurance.
jokes.setup: Mark talking to Andy about relationship advice
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic comment about seeking wisdom from Andy
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware joke about Andy being the worst person to ask for advice

jokes.text: Maybe if you had, I wouldn't have left.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his feelings about Justin
jokes.punchline: Suggesting their breakup was Ann's fault for talking about Justin
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's tendency to blame others for his own shortcomings

jokes.text: Well, frankly, I would like to hit that, but Ann's being a little weird about it.
jokes.setup: Tom asking why Leslie doesn't date Justin
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment using Tom's crude language
jokes.explanation: Leslie adopting Tom's crude language while being totally honest

jokes.text: Two, who is that hot intern chick out there? Because honestly, damn!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio in job interview with Ron
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate comment about April
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jean-Ralphio's inappropriate personality immediately

jokes.text: I want to punch you in the face so bad right now.
jokes.setup: Tom asking what Ron thinks of Jean-Ralphio
jokes.punchline: Ron's violent reaction to Tom's terrible choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction - direct and violent but controlled

jokes.text: If anyone comes to see you, I'll scare them away.
jokes.setup: April offering to be Ron's assistant
jokes.punchline: Her perfect understanding of what Ron actually wants
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing April understands Ron better than Tom's candidates

jokes.text: You're hired.
jokes.setup: April offering to scare people away
jokes.punchline: Ron's immediate decision without seeing the ties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - finding exactly what he needs in April's antisocial nature

jokes.text: I'm back on the horse, and this horse is a lawyer, so I'm looking forward to riding him. No, wait.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about dating again
jokes.punchline: Her accidental sexual innuendo and immediate regret
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie moment - innocent comment accidentally becoming sexual

jokes.text: Ten months ago, she was an intern who could give a C-R-A-P about local government. Now, she's our newest employee.
jokes.setup: Leslie toasting April's hiring
jokes.punchline: Her censoring herself by spelling out 'crap'
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's tendency to self-censor even mild language

jokes.text: Attagirl.
jokes.setup: April immediately blocking Jerry from seeing Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's proud approval of April's gatekeeping
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Ron's satisfaction with April as his human shield

tmdb_overview: Ann sets Leslie up on a blind date with a co-worker, which does not go as well as Leslie hoped. An old friend of Ann's makes Mark feel insecure.
insight: Romantic setup episode hits 74.8 with steady character work, light on standouts.
